topic: 大学生是否应更容易获得 SNAP 食品援助？
cont_clean: Illinois expects up to 150,000 people to lose food stamps this week alone. It is happening in every state in America. Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill expanded SNAP work requirements to adults up to age 64, eliminating exemptions that previously protected veterans, homeless people, and rural residents without access to employment. The new rule requires 80 hours of documented work, schooling, or volunteering every month to stay eligible. If you cannot prove it, you lose your benefits. States are already seeing the consequences. Louisiana has lost 20 percent of all SNAP beneficiaries according to the Center on Budget and Policy Priorities. Tennessee is down nearly 16 percent. Virginia down nearly 15 percent.
